name: dialog
description: A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content. Built on Radix UI with focus trap, escape handling, and scroll lock.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Default open state for uncontrolled usage

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Callback fired when open state changes: (open: boolean) => void

props.name: modal
props.description: When true, content outside the dialog is inert

examples.title: Basic dialog
examples.description: Dialog with title, description, and action buttons
examples.code: Edit profile Make changes to your profile here.

ai.whenToUse: Use for focused, interruptive tasks: confirmations, quick forms, detail views. The user must address the dialog before continuing.
ai.whenNotToUse: Don't use for destructive confirmations (use AlertDialog). Don't use for complex multi-step flows (use a full page). Don't use for non-critical info (use Tooltip or Popover).
commonMistakes: Forgetting DialogTitle (breaks accessibility — screen readers need it)
Using DialogDescription for long-form content (keep it short)
Putting too many primary actions in DialogFooter
ai.accessibilityNotes: Radix traps focus, handles Escape to close, and wires aria-labelledby/describedby to DialogTitle/DialogDescription. Always include a DialogTitle.
